text_original: need to thrive as adults.  Just as critically, it is also the time in which their brains develop – or fail to develop – in  a way that enables them to reach their full potential in life.  We already know that in the earliest years of childhood, children’s brains form neural  connections at a rate never to be repeated. This is the first window of opportunity  to influence the development of children’s brains through nutrition, stimulation and  protection from violence and other harms.
